leads.name: Sleeping Bear Press
leads.notes: Sleeping Bear Press is an Ann Arbor-based children’s book publisher — literally Cushing-Malloy’s neighbor — producing award-winning picture books and regional Michigan titles for over 30 years. Their active catalog of Michigan-themed children’s books signals consistent short-run full-color print needs. Geographic proximity creates an ideal partnership: in-person proofing, local relationship, same-city manufacturing.
leads.pitch: Call (866) 918-3956 or email submissions@sleepingbearpress.com. Lead with the Ann Arbor neighbor angle — two institutions literally down the street from each other. Ask about their 2026 title schedule and offer in-person proof review. The Michigan regional story is the perfect opening.

leads.name: Eerdmans Books for Young Readers
leads.notes: Eerdmans Books for Young Readers is the children’s imprint of William B. Eerdmans Publishing Company in Grand Rapids, MI — a 100-year-old press producing award-winning picture books and middle grade titles. Michigan-to-Michigan manufacturing is a natural conversation: 45 minutes from Ann Arbor, family-owned heritage on both sides. Active publishing calendar with consistent full-color children’s title production signals recurring print needs.
leads.pitch: Call (800) 253-7521 or email youngreaders@eerdmans.com. Lead with the Michigan manufacturing partnership angle: two family-owned Michigan institutions with deep heritage. Ask about their current vendor relationship and offer a comparison quote on an upcoming picture book title.

leads.name: New American Press
leads.notes: New American Press is an independent literary press in Milwaukee, WI publishing poetry and short fiction since 1998 — consistent catalog with 3–5 titles annually and an active contest program. Milwaukee to Ann Arbor is a natural Midwest corridor for a regional manufacturing partnership. David Schiff is the named contact and sole decision-maker, simplifying the sales process considerably. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email david@newamericanpress.com directly. Lead with the Midwest peer story — two independent operations building literary culture in the Great Lakes region. Ask about their 2026 contest winners and production calendar. Position Cushing-Malloy as the manufacturing partner who knows what a literary press needs.

leads.name: Two Dollar Radio
leads.notes: Two Dollar Radio is a Columbus, OH independent press known for provocative literary fiction and nonfiction — now operating as an active imprint following an April 2025 acquisition by Seven Stories Press, with Brett Petersen continuing to lead the imprint’s editorial direction. The brand’s identity is built on beautifully produced physical books, making offset printing quality a core value. Active title production signals an ongoing manufacturing need under the new arrangement.
leads.pitch: Email brett@twodollarradio.com or call (614) 725-1505. Reference Two Dollar Radio’s continued commitment to the physical book and position Cushing-Malloy as the Midwest manufacturing partner who can match that standard. Ask about Q2–Q3 titles and offer a comparison quote.

leads.name: Chelsea Cole (The Cookbook Lab)
leads.notes: Chelsea Cole runs The Cookbook Lab, a Portland-based food blog and cookbook resource with an established readership and a track record of self-publishing culinary titles. Her existing cookbook publishing history signals direct print buying decisions made without a traditional publisher intermediary. The Cookbook Lab’s business model is built on physical product sales to a loyal culinary audience, making her a natural candidate for a professional offset print upgrade. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email chelsea@thecookbooklab.com. Lead with her established cookbook publishing history. Position Cushing-Malloy as the manufacturing partner who can help her scale to offset quality at volume — offer a comparison quote at 500 vs. 1,000 copies to show her the per-unit economics. Tone should be collegial and cookbook-savvy.

leads.name: Atmosphere Press
leads.notes: Atmosphere Press is an Austin, TX hybrid publisher serving independent authors with full editorial, design, and production services — producing 50+ titles annually across nonfiction, fiction, and memoir. Their multi-client model makes them a multiplier account: every new author they sign is a potential Cushing-Malloy print run. Consistent volume and professional production standards make this a strategic manufacturing partnership opportunity.
leads.pitch: Call (740) 416-0443 or email books@atmospherepress.com. This is a manufacturing partnership pitch, not a single-title conversation. Position Cushing-Malloy as Atmosphere’s preferred offset printer: they handle editorial and author relationships, we manufacture. Ask about their current printing volume and discuss multi-title annual pricing.

leads.name: Arbutus Press
leads.notes: Arbutus Press is a Traverse City, MI independent publisher specializing in Michigan regional history, nature, and outdoors titles — consistent catalog production with a loyal regional readership. Michigan-to-Michigan is the entire story: two family-owned companies serving Michigan readers with Michigan-made books. Active catalog with 5–8 new titles annually across hiking guides, natural history, and Michigan nonfiction.
leads.pitch: Call (231) 946-7240 or email info@arbutuspress.com. Lead with the Michigan peer story — Traverse City to Ann Arbor, two independent Michigan presses committed to Michigan stories. Ask about their 2026 catalog and offer a quote on their next title. Regional pride will resonate immediately.

leads.name: Belt Publishing
leads.notes: Belt Publishing is a Cleveland, OH press that defined Rust Belt regional publishing — now operating as an active imprint following a 2024 acquisition by Arcadia Publishing, with continued independent editorial focus and a growing Great Lakes and Midwest nonfiction catalog. Their regional identity aligns perfectly with Cushing-Malloy’s Midwest manufacturing story. Active title acquisitions signal an ongoing print calendar with room for a new manufacturing relationship.
leads.pitch: Email query@beltpublishing.com or call (843) 853-2070. Lead with the Great Lakes connection: Cleveland to Ann Arbor, two institutions rooted in the industrial Midwest. Position Cushing-Malloy as the regional manufacturing partner for Belt’s continued growth. Ask about their 2026 acquisitions and production timeline.

leads.name: Curbside Splendor
leads.notes: Curbside Splendor is a Chicago-based indie press publishing contemporary fiction, essays, and poetry since 2012 — active catalog with a loyal readership and consistent production across multiple formats. Chicago to Ann Arbor is three hours, placing this squarely in Cushing-Malloy’s regional reach. Their diverse catalog signals varied trim sizes and specifications that benefit from a single dedicated manufacturing contact who knows their standards.
leads.pitch: Call (312) 855-2233 or email info@curbsidesplendor.com. Lead with the Midwest peer story and the practical advantage of a regional manufacturer. Offer a quote on their next title and position the single-contact model as the answer to coordination headaches small presses routinely face.

leads.name: Wolfson Press
leads.notes: Wolfson Press is a literary press operating out of Indiana University South Bend, publishing poetry and literary work with an active contest program and consistent title production. South Bend to Ann Arbor is under two hours — a natural Great Lakes corridor relationship. University-affiliated presses that manage their own printing relationships are solid accounts: institutional stability, recurring print cycles, and decision-makers who value a reliable regional partner. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email wolfson@iu.edu to introduce Cushing-Malloy. Reference their contest program and offer to quote the next winner’s print run. Position Cushing-Malloy as the experienced short-run poetry manufacturer. The Indiana-to-Michigan corridor is a natural geographic opening.

leads.name: FlowerSong Press
leads.notes: FlowerSong Press is a McAllen, TX independent press focused on Latinx voices in poetry and fiction — active contest program and consistent title production with a growing national literary reputation. Their Latinx literary mission demands production quality that honors the work, and POD limitations push many similar presses toward offset printing. Multiple titles annually and a growing reputation signal expanding print needs.
leads.pitch: Call (956) 739-2206 or email info@flowersongpress.com. Lead with Cushing-Malloy’s short-run poetry expertise and 20-day production timeline. Reference their active contest program and ask about the next winner’s production calendar.

leads.name: Deep Vellum
leads.notes: Deep Vellum is a Dallas, TX literary press specializing in world literature in translation — active title program with a strong national reputation and an integrated bookstore operation. Will Evans is the founder and publisher. Their translation catalog demands high-quality offset production standards that POD cannot match, and their growing platform with a physical retail presence signals increasing print volume. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email will@deepvellum.org directly. Lead with Deep Vellum’s commitment to the physical book — their bookstore reflects the same philosophy. Position Cushing-Malloy as the manufacturing partner who can deliver the offset quality that world literature in English deserves. Ask about their 2026 catalog.

leads.name: Spoonbridge Press
leads.notes: Spoonbridge Press is a Saint Paul, MN hybrid publisher serving independent authors with full editorial and production services — their multi-client model makes them a multiplier account where every author they sign is a potential Cushing-Malloy print run. Active production calendar in the Twin Cities, squarely in Cushing-Malloy’s Midwest reach. Minneapolis–Saint Paul’s robust literary community signals a growing pipeline of author clients.
leads.pitch: Call (651) 212-5242 or email hello@spoonbridgepress.com. Frame this as a manufacturing partnership, not a single-title pitch. Position Cushing-Malloy as Spoonbridge’s preferred printer: they manage the author relationship and editorial, we manufacture. Emphasize the single-contact model as the reliability upgrade their clients deserve.

leads.name: Dianne Jacob
leads.notes: Dianne Jacob is a San Francisco-based cookbook author, food writing instructor, and authority — author of ‘Will Write for Food,’ the definitive guide to food writing, now in multiple editions. Her platform as a cookbook writing instructor means she reaches hundreds of aspiring cookbook authors annually, making her both a direct prospect and a referral channel. Active in food writing communities with continued book project potential. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email dj@diannej.com directly. She knows the publishing process, so skip the basics. Get straight to offset quality, volume pricing, and timeline. Position Cushing-Malloy as both her preferred manufacturer and a resource she can recommend to the cookbook authors she teaches.

leads.name: C&R Press
leads.notes: C&R Press is a Winston-Salem, NC independent literary publisher with over a decade of consistent output in poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction — annual contest program generates recurring print needs with predictable cycles. C&R operates as a lean press where the founder manages production relationships directly, simplifying the sales conversation. Active 2026 title acquisitions signal an ongoing manufacturing need. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email accounts@crpress.org. Reference their contest program and consistent annual catalog. Position Cushing-Malloy as the reliable manufacturing partner for a lean independent press: single contact, 20-day production, consistent quality across every title.

leads.name: Sundress Publications
leads.notes: Sundress Publications is a Knoxville, TN nonprofit literary press focused on women writers and underrepresented voices in poetry and prose — active publishing program with multiple titles annually and a strong presence in the indie lit community. Their lean nonprofit structure means competitive short-run pricing is particularly valuable. Consistent production calendar with both full collections and chapbooks creates recurring print opportunities. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.
leads.pitch: Email sundresspublications@gmail.com. Lead with the short-run poetry manufacturing expertise and 20-day turnaround. Reference their chapbook and collection production specifically — Cushing-Malloy understands the difference, and that specificity will stand out.

leads.name: Brick Mantel Books
leads.notes: Brick Mantel Books is a St. Louis, MO independent poetry press — active since 2013 with a consistent chapbook and collection program serving Midwest and national poets. St. Louis to Ann Arbor is under five hours, making this a natural Midwest regional relationship. Their focus on affordable, quality poetry production aligns directly with Cushing-Malloy’s short-run offset capability at competitive pricing.
leads.pitch: Call (314) 827-6567 or email info@brickmantelbooks.com. Lead with the Midwest peer story and short-run offset manufacturing expertise. Position Cushing-Malloy as the regional manufacturing partner who understands poetry production at the scale a dedicated press needs.

leads.name: Shanti Arts Publishing
leads.notes: Shanti Arts Publishing is a Brunswick, ME press at the intersection of art, literature, and contemplative culture — producing poetry, art books, and literary titles with a distinctive aesthetic that demands premium printing quality. Active title production with a loyal audience in the literary arts community. Art-forward titles with photography and visual elements require the kind of careful paper selection and color management that only offset printing delivers.
leads.pitch: Call (207) 837-5760 or email info@shantiarts.com. Lead with Cushing-Malloy’s ability to produce art-forward books with offset quality — paper selection, color reproduction, binding standards that match Shanti Arts’ aesthetic. Ask about their next title and offer a quote showing the offset difference.

leads.name: Brandon Wilson (Be Wellthy)
leads.notes: Brandon Wilson is a health and wellness content creator operating the Be Wellthy brand on Instagram and YouTube, with an audience focused on functional fitness and intentional living. While a specific book project announcement was not confirmed, his structured program-based content translates naturally to a physical guide or book. Supplemental lead: lower score reflects the absence of a confirmed book announcement; his audience size, content format, and authority positioning make this a worthwhile outreach if he is in early-stage planning.
leads.pitch: Email bewellthybrandon@gmail.com. Keep the outreach brief and curiosity-based: acknowledge his content and ask whether a physical guide or book is something he has considered. Position Cushing-Malloy as the partner who makes the process easy for first-time health creators. Do not assume an active print run — open the door without pressure.

leads.name: Kayla Jeter
leads.notes: Kayla Jeter is a registered dietitian and health influencer with an active digital platform covering nutrition, gut health, and women’s wellness — her credentialed expertise and structured educational content are a natural fit for a print health guide or cookbook. While no specific book announcement was confirmed, her professional credibility and niche audience authority give this outreach strong conversion potential. Supplemental lead: lower score reflects absence of confirmed book intent; her RD credentials and content depth make a physical book a compelling next step for her brand.
leads.pitch: Email kayla@kaylajeter.com. Open with a reference to her nutrition and gut health content and ask whether she has considered putting her framework into a physical book or guide. Position Cushing-Malloy as the trusted printing partner for health professionals launching their first book.
